# UC-18.2.6 · Distributed Firewall Rule Hit Rate Analysis

## Description

Baselines hits per DFW rule and flags sudden drops (unused or bypassed) or spikes (attack or misconfiguration) — complements UC-18.2.1 volume view.

## Value

Baselines hits per DFW rule and flags sudden drops (unused or bypassed) or spikes (attack or misconfiguration) — complements UC-18.2.1 volume view.

## Implementation

Requires ≥7 days of data for baseline. Exclude ephemeral rules by lookup. Alert on zero-hit critical allow rules.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `vmware_nsx_addon`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=vmware:nsx:dfw`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Requires ≥7 days of data for baseline. Exclude ephemeral rules by lookup. Alert on zero-hit critical allow rules.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:nsx:dfw" earliest=-7d
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count by _time, rule_id, rule_name
| eventstats avg(count) as baseline by rule_id
| where count < baseline*0.2 OR count > baseline*5
| table _time, rule_id, rule_name, count, baseline
```

Understanding this SPL

**Distributed Firewall Rule Hit Rate Analysis** — Baselines hits per DFW rule and flags sudden drops (unused or bypassed) or spikes (attack or misconfiguration) — complements UC-18.2.1 volume view.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=vmware:nsx:dfw`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `vmware_nsx_addon`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vmware; **sourcetype**: vmware:nsx:dfw. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vmware, sourcetype="vmware:nsx:dfw", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time, rule_id, rule_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eventstats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by rule_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where count < baseline*0.2 OR count > baseline*5` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Distributed Firewall Rule Hit Rate Analysis**): table _time, rule_id, rule_name, count, baseline

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with VMware NSX Manager (monitoring, alarms, and relevant inventory) for the same time window.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (hits per rule), Table (anomalies), Heatmap (rule × day).

## SPL

```spl
index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:nsx:dfw" earliest=-7d
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count by _time, rule_id, rule_name
| eventstats avg(count) as baseline by rule_id
| where count < baseline*0.2 OR count > baseline*5
| table _time, rule_id, rule_name, count, baseline
```

## Visualization

Line chart (hits per rule), Table (anomalies), Heatmap (rule × day).
